exercises.title: を — Direct Object Marker
exercises.intro: を marks the thing an action is done TO — what you eat, drink, study, watch. Place it right after the object, just before the verb. Note: although written を, it is pronounced 'o' in modern Japanese.
rows: にほんごを べんきょうします
exercises.takeaway: を = direct object marker. Pattern: [OBJECT] を [VERB]. Always pronounced 'o'.

exercises.prompt: Which particle marks the direct object of an action verb (the thing you eat, drink, watch)?
Say 'I eat bread' using the direct object particle:

slot.hint: The particle that marks the direct object — what you do the verb TO.

exercises.speakText: パンを たべます。

exercises.prompt: Say 'I drink water every day' — fill in the object:
exercises.speakText: まいにち みずを のみます。

exercises.title: に — Multi-Purpose Particle
exercises.intro: に is the Swiss army knife of Japanese particles. Same little kana, four core jobs at N5 level: destination ('to'), specific time point ('at'), indirect object (the person you give/call), and existence location ('at/in' for います / あります).
rows: がっこうに いきます
しちじに おきます
ともだちに あげます
exercises.takeaway: に = destination, time point, recipient, or existence. Same kana, four jobs.

exercises.prompt: Which particle marks BOTH a destination ('to school') AND a specific time ('at 7')?
Say 'I go to school' — fill in the destination particle:

slot.hint: The particle that marks a destination or arrival point.

exercises.speakText: がっこうに いきます。

exercises.prompt: Say 'I wake up at 7' — fill in the time particle:

slot.hint: The particle attached to a specific clock time.

exercises.speakText: しちじに おきます。

exercises.title: へ — Direction (Toward)
exercises.intro: へ marks the direction of movement — the journey rather than the arrival. It pairs only with movement verbs (いきます, きます, かえります). Although written with the kana へ, it is pronounced 'e' as a particle. Softer and more literary than に.
rows: にほんへ いきます
にほんへ りょこうしたいです
I want to travel to Japan
みなみへ むかいます
exercises.takeaway: へ = direction with movement verbs. Pronounced 'e'. Slightly more literary than に.

exercises.prompt: Which particle emphasises DIRECTION ('toward [place]') with a movement verb?
Say 'I want to travel to Japan' — fill in the direction particle:

slot.hint: The particle for direction, pronounced like the vowel 'e'.

exercises.translation: I want to travel to Japan.
exercises.speakText: にほんへ りょこうしたいです。

exercises.title: を — Traversal (Moving Through)
exercises.intro: を has a second job beyond direct objects: with movement verbs like あるきます (walk), わたります (cross), and さんぽします (stroll), it marks the SPACE you move through. Think 'walk along', 'cross over', 'stroll through'.
rows: こうえんを さんぽします
はしを わたります
みちを あるきます
exercises.takeaway: を + movement verb = traversal. Marks the space you move THROUGH.

exercises.prompt: Which particle marks the SPACE you move through (e.g., 'walk through the park')?
Say 'I walk through the park' — fill in the traversal particle:

slot.hint: The same particle as direct-object — but here it marks the path of movement.

exercises.speakText: こうえんを さんぽします。

exercises.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'I eat bread.'?
exercises.title: を marks what you DO something TO

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.note: を marks the direct object of an action verb.

variants.label: COMMON MISTAKE
variants.note: が is used for liking/understanding, not for action verbs like たべる.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.note: に doesn't mark direct objects — use を for what you eat.

exercises.takeaway: Action verbs take を for their direct object — not が, not に.
exercises.speakText: パンを たべます。

exercises.prompt: Which sentence is correct for 'I like sushi.'?
exercises.title: すき takes が, not を

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.note: すきです is a state, not an action — the liked thing takes が.

variants.label: COMMON MISTAKE
variants.note: すき (like), わかる (understand), できる (can do) all use が, never を.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.note: に doesn't mark the thing liked — only が works with すき.

exercises.takeaway: すき / わかる / できる take が, not を. They describe states, not actions.
exercises.speakText: すしが すきです。

exercises.prompt: Which is the natural way to say 'I go to school'?
exercises.title: に vs へ — destination vs direction

variants.label: NATURAL
variants.jp: がっこうに いきます。
variants.note: に focuses on the destination — most common in everyday speech.

variants.label: NATURAL
variants.jp: がっこうへ いきます。
variants.note: へ also works — softer, more literary, emphasises direction.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: がっこうを いきます。
variants.note: を doesn't mark a destination — use に or へ with いきます.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: がっこうで いきます。
variants.note: で marks where an action happens, not a destination.

exercises.takeaway: に and へ both work for 'go to'. Only に works for time, indirect object, and existence.
exercises.speakText: がっこうに いきます。

exercises.prompt: Which correctly says 'I want to travel to Japan' — emphasising the journey?
exercises.title: へ for direction with りょこう / むかいます

variants.label: NATURAL
variants.jp: にほんに りょこうしたいです。
variants.en: I want to travel to Japan.
variants.note: に naturally marks Japan as the destination for this travel sentence.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.jp: にほんを りょこうしたいです。
variants.en: I want to travel around Japan.
variants.note: 日本を旅行する is grammatical, but it means traveling through/around Japan, not simply going to Japan.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.jp: にほんで りょこうしたいです。
variants.en: I want to travel inside Japan.
variants.note: で changes the meaning to 'travel WITHIN Japan' — not what you said.

exercises.takeaway: に marks the destination; を with 旅行する can mean traveling through or around a place.
exercises.speakText: にほんに りょこうしたいです。

exercises.title: Sentences in Context
exercises.intro: These sentences combine を, に, and へ as you'll meet them in real life. Tap the speaker on each row to hear it.
rows: まいにち みずを のみます。
にほんごを べんきょうします。
あした とうきょうに いきます。
I will go to Tokyo tomorrow.
くじに がっこうに いきます。
ははに でんわを します。
つくえの うえに ほんが あります。
tsukue no ue ni hon ga arimasu.
There is a book on the desk.
にほんへ りょこうしたいです。
I want to travel to Japan.
こうえんを さんぽします。
exercises.takeaway: Mix を, に, and へ freely — each does its own job in the same sentence.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: まいにち みずを のみます。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: がっこうに いきます。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: しちじに おきます。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: にほんへ りょこうしたいです。
options: I want to travel to Japan.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: こうえんを さんぽします。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: ともだちに あげます。
options: I give it to a friend.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: つくえの うえに ほんが あります。
options: I read a book at the desk.
The desk is under the book.
There is a book on the desk.
I put the book on the desk.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: にほんごを べんきょうします。

exercises.speakText: パンを たべます

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: みずを のみます

exercises.speakText: がっこうに いきます

exercises.speakText: こうえんを さんぽします

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its meaning:

exercises.title: Reading practice — を, に, へ in action

cards.jp: にほんごを べんきょうします。
がっこうに いきます。
しちじに おきます。
ともだちに あげます。
cards.en: I give it to a friend.

cards.jp: つくえの うえに ほんが あります。
cards.romaji: tsukue no ue ni hon ga arimasu.
cards.en: There is a book on the desk.

cards.jp: にほんへ りょこうしたいです。
cards.en: I want to travel to Japan.

cards.jp: こうえんを さんぽします。
ははに でんわを します。
